an1.316:1.1: "One person, *bhikkhus*, arising in the world, arises for the harm of many, for the unhappiness of many, for the loss, harm, and *dukkha* of many, of gods and humans.
an1.316:1.3: One who holds wrong view, who sees things in a distorted way.
an1.316:1.4: Such a one leads many people away from the true *dhamma* and establishes them in the false *dhamma*.
an1.316:1.5: This, *bhikkhus*, is the one person who, arising in the world, arises for the harm of many, for the unhappiness of many, for the loss, harm, and *dukkha* of many, of gods and humans."
an1.317:1.1: "One person, *bhikkhus*, arising in the world, arises for the welfare of many, for the happiness of many, for the good, welfare, and *sukha* of many, of gods and humans.
an1.317:1.3: One who holds right view, who sees things without distortion.
an1.317:1.4: Such a one leads many people away from the false *dhamma* and establishes them in the true *dhamma*.
an1.317:1.5: This, *bhikkhus*, is the one person who, arising in the world, arises for the welfare of many, for the happiness of many, for the good, welfare, and *sukha* of many, of gods and humans."
an1.318:1.1: "I see no other single *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, that is as greatly blameworthy as this: wrong view.
an1.318:1.2: Wrong view, *bhikkhus*, is the foremost of greatly blameworthy things."
an1.319:1.1: "I see no other single person, *bhikkhus*, who has so practiced for the harm of many, for the unhappiness of many, for the loss, harm, and *dukkha* of many, of gods and humans, as Makkhalī the fool.
an1.319:1.2: Just as, *bhikkhus*, a fish-trap set at the mouth of a river would be for the harm, *dukkha*, ruin, and disaster of many fish;
an1.319:1.3: so too, *bhikkhus*, Makkhalī the fool is like a human fish-trap, arisen in the world for the harm, *dukkha*, ruin, and disaster of many beings."
an1.320:1.1: "In a poorly taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], both the one who encourages and the one who is encouraged, and the one who, having been encouraged, practices accordingly — all of them accumulate much demerit.
an1.320:1.3: Because the *dhamma* has been poorly taught, *bhikkhus*."
an1.321:1.1: "In a well-taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], both the one who encourages and the one who is encouraged, and the one who, having been encouraged, practices accordingly — all of them accumulate much merit.
an1.322:1.1: "In a poorly taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], measure should be known by the giver, not by the receiver.
an1.322:1.3: Because the *dhamma* has been poorly taught, *bhikkhus*."
an1.323:1.1: "In a well-taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], measure should be known by the receiver, not by the giver.
an1.324:1.1: "In a poorly taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], one who is energetic dwells in *dukkha*.
an1.324:1.3: Because the *dhamma* has been poorly taught, *bhikkhus*."
an1.325:1.1: "In a well-taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], one who is lazy dwells in *dukkha*.
an1.326:1.1: "In a poorly taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], one who is lazy dwells in *sukha*.
an1.326:1.3: Because the *dhamma* has been poorly taught, *bhikkhus*."
an1.327:1.1: "In a well-taught *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*], one who is energetic dwells in *sukha*.
an1.328:1.1: "Just as even a small amount of excrement is foul-smelling, *bhikkhus*;
an1.328:1.2: so too I do not praise even a small amount of existence, *bhikkhus* — not even the duration of a finger-snap."
an1.329:1.1: "Just as even a small amount of urine is foul-smelling, *bhikkhus* … even a small amount of spittle is foul-smelling … even a small amount of pus is foul-smelling … even a small amount of blood is foul-smelling;
an1.329:1.2: so too I do not praise even a small amount of existence, *bhikkhus* — not even the duration of a finger-snap."
